libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: For version 2.0.0, generate tests using the jakarta.activation package names, not the pre-2.0 javax.activation imports from the existing 1.2.1 tests. Prefer covering public Jakarta Activation usage such as CommandInfo/CommandObject, DataHandler/DataSource, MailcapCommandMap, and MimetypesFileTypeMap.

deterministic_setup.reason: Provides the com.sun.activation.registries implementation classes and default mailcap/mimetypes resources required by normal Jakarta Activation runtime paths that the API jar references but does not include.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8769 [Automation] javac compile fails for jakarta.activation:jakarta.activation-api:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: com.sun.activation:jakarta.activation:2.0.0 also contains jakarta.activation classes, so the test classpath will contain duplicate API classes; keep the target jakarta.activation-api dependency present and use the implementation dependency only to supply missing runtime support classes/resources.
Local Gradle and Native Image verification remain authoritative because API-only paths that avoid mailcap/mimetype registries may compile without the implementation but provide limited coverage.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Jakarta Activation API 2.0.0 is a compile/API artifact; its POM states it omits implementation-specific classes, while key normal-use classes such as MailcapCommandMap and MimetypesFileTypeMap reference com.sun.activation.registries classes that are not present in the API jar. Meaningful runtime coverage of MIME/mailcap behavior therefore needs the matching implementation artifact on the test classpath.
